summary: Decompiled the melee lbtime module by replacing asm/melee/lb/lbtime.o with src/melee/lb/lbtime.o, adding lbtime.c/lbtime.h, and introducing the Dolphin OSCalendarTime type needed by time conversion code. The PR also renamed the SDK symbol func_8034C668 to OSTicksToCalendarTime across assembly references and declarations. Most lbtime functions were expressed in C, while func_8000AFBC remained as inline assembly because the PR author noted scheduling issues. Build metadata was updated for Epilogue/obj file routing, including an lbtime-specific MWCC version override path fix.
what_changed: Added src/melee/lb/lbtime.c with C implementations for func_8000AEC8, func_8000AEE4, func_8000AF24, func_8000AF74, and func_8000B028.
Kept func_8000AFBC as inline assembly in lbtime.c, with the C attempt commented out and annotated as nonmatching due to scheduling silliness.
Added src/melee/lb/lbtime.h declaring lbtime functions plus external OSTicksToCalendarTime, OSGetTime, and __div2i.
Added include/dolphin/os/os.h containing the OSCalendarTime struct with sec, min, hour, mday, mon, year, wday, yday, msec, and usec fields.
Renamed the Dolphin SDK assembly label func_8034C668 to OSTicksToCalendarTime and updated callers in code_80016A54.s, lbsnap.s, and lbtime.s.
Updated include/functions.h comment/declaration list from func_8034C668 to OSTicksToCalendarTime.
Switched obj_files.mk from building asm/melee/lb/lbtime.o to src/melee/lb/lbtime.o.
Added lbtime.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
Changed the lbtime-specific Makefile MWCC_EPI_VERSION override path from src/lb/lbtime.o to src/melee/lb/lbtime.o.
Set GENERATE_MAP default from 1 to 0 and changed MWCC_EPI_VERSION assignment inside the Epilogue block from := to =.
smart_moves: Used a named SDK function, OSTicksToCalendarTime, instead of preserving the anonymous func_8034C668 label, making lbtime and other call sites more understandable.
Introduced OSCalendarTime in a Dolphin OS header rather than embedding an ad hoc struct in lbtime, allowing reuse by other OS time code.
Preserved matching progress by writing most of lbtime in C while isolating the known hard scheduling case, func_8000AFBC, as inline assembly.
Updated all visible assembly call-site comments/labels for the renamed SDK function, avoiding split naming between C declarations and asm references.
Fixed the object-list handoff so the build now pulls lbtime from src/melee/lb/lbtime.c rather than the previous assembly object.
decomp_lessons: When a module mostly decompiles but one function is blocked by instruction scheduling, inline assembly can be used as a temporary bridge while still landing the surrounding C translation.
SDK functions should be renamed to canonical names once recognized, and all asm callers plus shared function listings should be updated together.
Adding a real SDK struct definition can unblock cleaner function signatures and reduce opaque pointer usage in newly decompiled code.
Build overrides must use the exact object path after source tree layout changes; here the lbtime MWCC_EPI_VERSION override needed src/melee/lb/lbtime.o.
Unsigned saturation helpers in this module use boundary checks against -1/UINT_MAX, 0xffff, and 0xff; future work should preserve these saturation semantics when renaming or refactoring.
naming_conventions: Recognized Dolphin SDK functions should use official SDK-style names such as OSTicksToCalendarTime instead of autogenerated func_8034C668 labels.
Dolphin OS time structs use the OS-prefixed CamelCase convention, e.g. OSCalendarTime.
The added lbtime header used a lowercase include guard form _lbtime_h_, matching the file name rather than a project-wide uppercase guard in this slice.
Unidentified melee-local functions remained as address-based func_8000AEC8-style names, indicating no semantic names were asserted without evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: func_8000AFBC had a commented C equivalent using OSGetTime divided by the bus/core timebase value at 0x800000F8 shifted by 2, capped at UINT_MAX, but the actual implementation was inline asm to preserve scheduling.
The inline assembly for func_8000AFBC explicitly calls OSGetTime and __div2i, manages r30/r31, and branches to lbl_8000B00C for the saturation path.
func_8000B028 compiles as a small wrapper around OSTicksToCalendarTime, multiplying ticks by the timebase-derived factor from 0x800000F8 >> 2.
The Makefile includes an lbtime-specific MWCC_EPI_VERSION override to 1.2.5, suggesting compiler-version selection was part of matching this object.
lbtime.o was added to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED, consistent with the PR body note: 'todo: fix the scheduling of func_8000AFBC'.
review_feedback: No review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body contains the only explicit caveat: 'todo: fix the scheduling of func_8000AFBC'.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fix the scheduling of func_8000AFBC or replace its inline assembly with matching C.
Search for later changes to include/dolphin/os/os.h to see whether OSCalendarTime gained include guards or additional OS time declarations.
Search for further SDK symbol renames from func_8034Cxxx to canonical Dolphin OS names.
Check whether the Makefile GENERATE_MAP default change was intentional for this PR or incidental.
